name: Voter Action Project Team
subject: Fight back against voter suppression
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
These state-level bills -- based on the suppressive SAVE America Act, which is blocked in the Senate -- will harm voter turnout and block eligible voters from casting valid ballots. Our efforts to protect the fundamental right to vote depend on stopping them, one state at a time.
Fuel our work to defend voting rights with $35 or whatever you can right now >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=051126_2&amount=35]
Voter Action Project[https://voteractionproject.org/]
Democracy Docket: As SAVE America Act stalls, GOP states are quietly enacting their own proof of citizenship laws [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=051126_2&amount=35]
When the House rammed the SAVE America Act (the new version of the SAVE Act) to passage earlier this year, we turned to the Senate to stop it. So far that effort has been successful, but that doesn't mean the threat is over.
State by state, opponents of voting rights in state legislatures around the country are pushing their own version of this suppressive legislation ahead of the midterm elections , folks.
Ten states have laws -- four of which passed during this second Trump administration -- that require proof of citizenship when registering to vote. Florida, Mississippi, South Dakota and Utah all enacted proof of citizenship voting bills in the past two weeks . In as many as 12 other states, bills are moving through state legislatures. If they all pass, that would mean nearly half the states in our country have passed mini versions of the SAVE America Act .
Defenders of voting rights know that non-citizen voting is already illegal, extremely rare, and that these bills are written in ways that could disenfranchise millions of eligible women across the country. That hasn't stopped anti-voting groups from pressuring states to pass these new restrictive voting laws, because the suppression is their goal.
Please, Team, we need to stop this dangerous legislation wherever we can. Pitch in with $35 or whatever you can and ensure millions of women's voices continue to be heard in our elections >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=051126_2&amount=35]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=051126_2&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=051126_2&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=051126_2&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=051126_2&amount=100] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=051126_2&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=051126_2&amount=35]
Opponents of voting rights in Congress, state legislatures, governor's mansions, and in the White House favor bills like the SAVE America Act because they know it could suppress millions of eligible voters, changing the composition of the electorate and potentially empowering them and their party for years to come.
These bills are all being fast-tracked by vote suppressors to be in place before November, folks. This movement is a stunning assault on our fundamental right to vote, and we can not afford to let this kind of anti-democratic legislation become law.
Catherine Engelbrecht, the founder of suppressive organization True the Vote, described the right-wing conspiracy theory group’s focus during a recent livestream: “ If the SAVE America Act isn’t going to pass then I’m going to use every tool at my disposal to try and get states to react to something ,” Engelbrecht said.
These dark-money suppression groups are willing to go state-by-state to enact their agenda and we have no choice but to match them with our grassroots power.
The consequences of these bills could be catastrophic for our democracy. Chip in with $35 or whatever you can today to stop more of these voter suppression bills from becoming law >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=051126_2&amount=35]
https://voteractionproject.org/ [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=051126_2&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=051126_2&amount=20]
Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code.
A copy of Integrity First Voter Action Project's latest financial report may be obtained by mail at Integrity First Voter Action Project, c/o Mele Brengarth & Assoc, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003.

clean_body: Did you miss question #2? | | , I know you usually respond right away - that's why I was surprised not to see your May MAGA Survey yet. Maybe you overlooked Question #2? Question #2 is the ONLY required question because it's critical. So, if you're low on time, can you at least answer #2? I'm counting on you. name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: This is why so many of our volunteers speak both English and Spanish, especially in border communities like El Paso.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople
body:
# [](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/43706?t=1&akid=240841%2E444211%2E1Xwexb)
I joined our El Paso Powered by People team on the UTEP campus last week and had a chance to meet Vanessa.
When I greeted her in English, she responded in Spanish and so I continued the conversation in her preferred language.
Like a lot of students in border campuses (from the Rio Grande Valley, up through Laredo, Eagle Pass, Del Rio and all the way to El Paso), Vanessa speaks both English and Spanish.
But when it comes to personal issues she, like many of her fellow students, prefers to speak in her native language.
That’s why so many of our volunteers speak both English and Spanish, especially in border communities like El Paso.
It’s our chance to listen to these students and make a real, personal connection with them. It’s critical to our work to register and work with them to ensure they vote in the upcoming midterm elections.
That’s how we were able to register Vanessa and agree with her that we’d stay in touch through the election to answer any questions she has about voting and to encourage her to cast a ballot for the first time in her life.
[**Your support gives us the means to meet young Texans wherever they are, whatever language they speak. Thank you for making a donation to support this important work.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20260511.Iz_Bzr&refcode=ema20260511-240841&t=3&akid=240841%2E444211%2E1Xwexb)

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end.

But when it comes to personal issues she, like many of her fellow students, prefers to speak in her native language. That’s why so many of our volunteers speak both English and Spanish, especially in border communities like El Paso. It’s our chance to listen to these students and make a real, personal connection with them. It’s critical to our work to register and work with them to ensure they vote in the upcoming midterm elections. That’s how we were able to register Vanessa and agree with her that we’d stay in touch through the election to answer any questions she has about voting and to encourage her to cast a ballot for the first time in her life. Beto

name: Justice Democrats
subject: Last week’s Change the Party rally →
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

In case you missed it, Justice Democrats came together in San Francisco
last week to rally, and it was epic!

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260511-2.VqHipA&refcode=em20260511-37768&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=37768%2E1203867%2E_B9PCN ]Photo of Saikat Chakrabarti, other Justice Democrats candidates, and
supporters on stage at the Change the Party rally

JD executive director Alexandra Rojas joined former Congressman Jamaal
Bowman, streamer and activist Hasan Piker, and 2026 JD primary candidates
Melat Kiros (CO-01), Darializa Avila Chevalier (NY-13), and Angela
Gonzalez-Torres (CA-34) to support Saikat Chakrabarti in his race to fill
Nancy Pelosi’s open house seat in CA-11.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260511-2.VqHipA&refcode=em20260511-37768&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1003&akid=37768%2E1203867%2E_B9PCN ]Photo of the packed crowd at the Change the Party rally.

The room was absolutely packed, with people chanting “Abolish ICE,” “Tax
the Rich,” and “Medicare for All.” The only way we’ll do those things is
by changing the Democratic Party, and we need each other to do it.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260511-2.VqHipA&refcode=em20260511-37768&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1004&akid=37768%2E1203867%2E_B9PCN ]Photo of Jamaal Bowman, Angela Gonzalez-Torres, Darializa Avila
Chevalier, Melat Kiros, Hasan Piker, Saikat Chakrabarti, and Alexandra
Rojas.

As Saikat said, our work has always been about people-powered movements,
and this time, it's not just one of us at the top of a presidential
ticket. This year, we have more than a dozen candidates running across the
country, ready to change the party from within.

From California to Colorado to New York, our communities are connected by
our shared struggles, which is why our progressive candidates are united
in the fight to take this party back so we can take our country back for
working families.

None of our candidates takes corporate PAC money, so they all need a
groundswell of grassroots support to defeat their establishment-backed
opponents and win their competitive Democratic primaries in the coming
weeks and months.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260511-2.VqHipA&refcode=em20260511-37768&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1005&akid=37768%2E1203867%2E_B9PCN ]We can’t do it without you,
Peter. Will you pitch in $5 or
more to directly fuel our work to elect Saikat, Melat, Darializa, Angela,
and other progressive champions to Congress?

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260511-2.VqHipA&refcode=em20260511-37768&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1024&akid=37768%2E1203867%2E_B9PCN ]Contribute $5

Every single donation, no matter how small or big, genuinely makes an
impact and allows our organization to support the kind of leaders our
communities deserve.

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20260511-37768-footer&t=1025&refcode2=37768_1203867__B9PCN&akid=37768%2E1203867%2E_B9PCN

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1026&akid=37768%2E1203867%2E_B9PCN

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
Justice Democrats
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

name: Mallory McMorrow
subject: Ted Cruz
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McMorrow for Michigan
body:
# [](https://act.mcmorrowformichigan.com/go/685?refcode=&t=1&akid=4690%2E898137%2E1riwJ2)
In the last few weeks, the Trump campaign, the Heritage Foundation, and even _Ted Cruz_ have attacked me and my campaign.
**That means they see us as a threat. Good — they should.**
Polls show I’m the best candidate to take on Mike Rogers. I’ve grown the most in the polls since we launched last year. The momentum we have is real.
_Now, we need to capitalize on the moment, fight back against the GOP attacks, and win this race._
I’m not accepting a cent of corporate PAC money. But between these attacks, right-wing ads running against me, and more than $45 million in super PAC money pouring into Michigan to prop up Rogers’ campaign, I need your help.
[**Can you donate $10 to help us fight back against people like Ted Cruz and, more importantly, win this race?**](https://act.mcmorrowformichigan.com/go/6442?amount=10&t=2&akid=4690%2E898137%2E1riwJ2)

Thanks.
\- Mallory

name: Sarah Kline
subject: Peter, we’re in some tight electoral races this year
body:
https://secure.ngpvan.com/2aYH7oLMDUGCzowlqvcGrQ2?am=20&amtOpts=&recurringAm=20&recurringAmtOpts=&is_optimized_ask=true&contactdata=YGJJuoPz0q91S+VqzfKWS8LAuGXvcLYpe3EHDm6JWwvDMPPO8wWxgBjzr7Zgt1quOF+AvNX0YUw0Q+HlX4RtJ+T2ECzdkdlio2EGdThSebZA9VsIxCU2ndhdQzUk/JL4 [https://secure.ngpvan.com/2aYH7oLMDUGCzowlqvcGrQ2?am=20&amtOpts=&recurringAm=20&recurringAmtOpts=&is_optimized_ask=true&contactdata=YGJJuoPz0q91S+VqzfKWS8LAuGXvcLYpe3EHDm6JWwvDMPPO8wWxgBjzr7Zgt1quOF+AvNX0YUw0Q+HlX4RtJ+T2ECzdkdlio2EGdThSebZA9VsIxCU2ndhdQzUk/JL4]
Hi Peter,
The 2026 primary election is coming up next week on Tuesday, May 19th. Our endorsements matter so much this year, not only because we were more selective for our climate champions, but also because several of our endorsements reflect races where we have an opportunity to switch the seat for a pro-climate candidate.
Several of these races, such as Myrna Muñoz for Senate District 15, are looking tight in this last week. With such little time left, we are pushing ahead with full steam to ensure that our ballots for the November election are full of pro-environment leaders.
That’s why we’re asking for your help. Would you consider making a gift of $50 or $100 to OLCV’s PAC today so we can fund the last meter of this race? [https://secure.ngpvan.com/2aYH7oLMDUGCzowlqvcGrQ2]
If you’re eligible, you can use Oregon’s Political Tax Credit to get your gift back when you file your taxes! You are eligible for Oregon’s Political Tax Credit if:
✔ You are an Oregon resident
✔ You will submit an Oregon tax return for 2026
✔ You make less than $75,000 (individual) or $150,000 (couple filing jointly)
Donate now [https://secure.ngpvan.com/2aYH7oLMDUGCzowlqvcGrQ2?am=20&amtOpts=&recurringAm=20&recurringAmtOpts=&is_optimized_ask=true&contactdata=YGJJuoPz0q91S+VqzfKWS8LAuGXvcLYpe3EHDm6JWwvDMPPO8wWxgBjzr7Zgt1quOF+AvNX0YUw0Q+HlX4RtJ+T2ECzdkdlio2EGdThSebZA9VsIxCU2ndhdQzUk/JL4]
This moment matters the most for which pro-climate candidates we’ll have a chance to elect this fall, so I’m humbly asking for your support now. Please consider making your gift today for bold climate action in the next legislative session! [https://secure.ngpvan.com/2aYH7oLMDUGCzowlqvcGrQ2]
And don’t forget – if you’re voting by mail, tomorrow is the recommended deadline* to mail your ballot in to ensure it gets counted!
Let’s do this,
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/AV/AVLCV/1/70877/images/Sarah%20Signature.png]
Sarah Kline (she/they)
OLCV Communications & Membership Coordinator
*Due to federal changes to USPS, mail may not be postmarked on the day that it is received by the post office, so the Secretary of State has made this recommendation. You may still bring your ballot to the post office and request a post mark, or take your ballot to an official dropbox [https://sos.oregon.gov/voting/Pages/drop-box-locator.aspx] by 8:00 PM on May 19th.
https://www.facebook.com/oregonlcv [https://www.facebook.com/oregonlcv] https://bsky.app/profile/olcv.org [https://bsky.app/profile/olcv.org] https://www.instagram.com/olcv/?next=%2F [https://www.instagram.com/olcv/?next=%2F] https://x.com/OLCV [https://x.com/OLCV]

Oregon League of Conservation Voters
1110 SE Alder Suite 220
Portland, OR 97214
United States

name: Sheriff Joe Arpaio
subject: In prison for saving lives? Not on our watch
body:
Patriot,
This is a disgrace...and every American who believes in law and order should be furious.
Two police officers—Josh Taylor and Brandon Dingman—are currently serving prison sentences for doing exactly what they were trained and ordered to do: respond to a dangerous call, use non-lethal force, and protecting their community.
Let me tell you what really happened, because the media and the District Attorney certainly won't.
On the night of July 4th, 2019, Officer Josh Taylor responded to a domestic disturbance call in Wilson, Oklahoma. When he arrived, he encountered Jared Lakey: a large, fully grown man walking naked down the street wearing nothing but socks.
Josh immediately recognized this situation for what it was: dangerous and unpredictable. Whether it was drugs, mental illness, or something else—this wasn't a man in his right mind. And Josh knew he couldn't handle it alone. He called for backup.
[Donate to Their Defense](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=3d2d27084ff54125a57d5e50c6be433c&_e=pSQQMevn8hkaGXIpsLXSNlVSMvc64oS45SK7H89wlpI7iuUxZ62TLNjPVPOfvdBM2i5XVqcJuMaTj4gk9LPmRKKUhE75eowjy5or9V25zth1HG-kPC8CFhlPfbfQVevH-PLF3rLmsqvSxZ5l1_j-dPaHXsQNB7J7fKJWzoj1YwXHac0-aGBp0vePzQwozRMqSZhMBAXLS790jpY2CnMsv9cbflU4oHnjY_Ddf_6VVgDS8DjHEDxC_I_EhIa-1gEgWkiUHrQ1G6svCieFYz3Nl2kYD-uw4hStTvOohOeukH1GAI4WnQDHji_GAqPlMYkah-BMF9d3Cb9bNPKcQjgYiArdPhNiDaH7oPVFa38ad0cnMW3Rf_L4zdJNZyYv_hhthIqdBW7F7FkY4QdR9m7sxYinaB4_BAFnUV_rd11LxVAIOnlgYpFdbR1OWn3jJ--h)
Officer Josh Taylor
Officer Brandon Dingman
Officer Brandon Dingman arrived on scene, and the two officers tried to do what they were trained to do: safely and professionally bring Mr. Lakey into custody.
But Lakey was 350 pounds, combative, and resisting arrest. When verbal commands didn't work and physical restraint proved impossible, they turned to their department-issued, non-lethal tasers.
They deployed them multiple times, but Lakey kept tearing the barbs out. He was clearly not being fully affected by the tasers—and even after repeated attempts, the officers were unable to subdue him.
Eventually, Josh and Brandon were able to get his arms and cuffed him. They immediately called for medical assistance. An ambulance arrived. Lakey was taken to the hospital.
At that point, the officers had done everything they could—and everything right.
But tragically, Jared Lakey passed away two days later. The autopsy was inconclusive. He had an enlarged heart and lung issues. Drug screenings were unreliable due to the fluids pumped into his system at the hospital. There was no determined cause of death.
Even so, the department launched an internal review. Then the City Council reviewed the case too.
Both cleared the officers. That's two separate investigations concluding Josh and Brandon followed procedure.
Case closed, right?
Wrong.
A full year later, a politically motivated District Attorney, smelling an opportunity, dragged the case back up and charged these officers with second-degree murder.
Let that sink in: second-degree murder. He ignored the internal findings. He ignored the medical uncertainty. He ignored the fact that these officers never even laid a hand on Lakey's neck or airway.
They followed their training, used the gear they were issued, and even called for medical help.
But none of that mattered to a DA looking to make headlines.
Now, these two good cops, family men, community protectors, are sitting behind bars.
The National Center for Police Defense is fighting to overturn this injustice. We're supporting their legal appeals, helping their families, and backing efforts to remove the biased District Attorney who never should have touched this case in the first place.
But appeals are costly. And the longer this drags on, the longer Josh and Brandon sit in prison...separated from their families, their children, their lives.
That's why I'm writing you today. We need your help.
[Yes — I'll Donate Today](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=3d2d27084ff54125a57d5e50c6be433c&_e=pSQQMevn8hkaGXIpsLXSNlVSMvc64oS45SK7H89wlpI7iuUxZ62TLNjPVPOfvdBM2i5XVqcJuMaTj4gk9LPmRKKUhE75eowjy5or9V25ztgIDtn_QAByoaomg2yVbooTwiDqYNgPFWfR3tguer_Dz_fNgJHTFX6Y0wXo_e3R6mUr4uh7j5esasiRImqTr4NSvUEdQ06u_M-ccX79Yj60Oxqop1I8Zitxsj97cQcdL5UOEKIV8t9e5rfWyxS_RlSSLaRHzImAS3Ol9lSCdk3ysBd2fKKATrjUP_tyvHIeJR9zh7MuzTO_uHZh8yda6JrJxDdI0NM25lD4ey9ZPpZepTvoaP1qBZw5xhYQUYjo796dNl3JG3lmb2h_A3tAOp4TZ49nuTolysX7UDkp-PeIgoYoCsPK6y0S7cmc7v44TIiG8a4zq1Fs7hTygqc-KrZ2)
We need to raise funds to fuel the legal defense and appeal process. These officers deserve their freedom. They deserve a fair hearing. And they deserve to come home.
We cannot let this go unanswered. If we don't stand up now, more and more police officers will suffer the same fate: thrown to the wolves by political activists in prosecutor's clothing.
Josh and Brandon were railroaded, plain and simple. And now we need to right this wrong.
Will you step up today and donate to support their appeals?

Every dollar goes directly to their legal defense and appeals. Help bring them home.

clean_body: Patriot, This is a disgrace...and every American who believes in law and order should be furious. Two police officers—Josh Taylor and Brandon Dingman—are currently serving prison sentences for doing exactly what they were trained and ordered to do: respond to a dangerous call, use non-lethal force, and protecting their community. Let me tell you what really happened, because the media and the District Attorney certainly won't. On the night of July 4th, 2019, Officer Josh Taylor responded to a domestic disturbance call in Wilson, Oklahoma. When he arrived, he encountered Jared Lakey: a large, fully grown man walking naked down the street wearing nothing but socks. Josh immediately recognized this situation for what it was: dangerous and unpredictable. Whether it was drugs, mental illness, or something else—this wasn't a man in his right mind. And Josh knew he couldn't handle it alone. He called for backup. Officer Josh Taylor Officer Brandon Dingman Officer Brandon Dingman arrived on scene, and the two officers tried to do what they were trained to do: safely and professionally bring Mr. Lakey into custody. But Lakey was 350 pounds, combative, and resisting arrest. When verbal commands didn't work and physical restraint proved impossible, they turned to their department-issued, non-lethal tasers. They deployed them multiple times, but Lakey kept tearing the barbs out. He was clearly not being fully affected by the tasers—and even after repeated attempts, the officers were unable to subdue him. Eventually, Josh and Brandon were able to get his arms and cuffed him. They immediately called for medical assistance. An ambulance arrived. Lakey was taken to the hospital. At that point, the officers had done everything they could—and everything right. But tragically, Jared Lakey passed away two days later. The autopsy was inconclusive. He had an enlarged heart and lung issues. Drug screenings were unreliable due to the fluids pumped into his system at the hospital. There was no determined cause of death. Even so, the department launched an internal review. Then the City Council reviewed the case too. Both cleared the officers. That's two separate investigations concluding Josh and Brandon followed procedure. Case closed, right? Wrong. A full year later, a politically motivated District Attorney, smelling an opportunity, dragged the case back up and charged these officers with second-degree murder. Let that sink in: second-degree murder. He ignored the internal findings. He ignored the medical uncertainty. He ignored the fact that these officers never even laid a hand on Lakey's neck or airway. They followed their training, used the gear they were issued, and even called for medical help. But none of that mattered to a DA looking to make headlines. Now, these two good cops, family men, community protectors, are sitting behind bars. The National Center for Police Defense is fighting to overturn this injustice. We're supporting their legal appeals, helping their families, and backing efforts to remove the biased District Attorney who never should have touched this case in the first place. But appeals are costly. And the longer this drags on, the longer Josh and Brandon sit in prison...separated from their families, their children, their lives. That's why I'm writing you today. We need your help. We need to raise funds to fuel the legal defense and appeal process. These officers deserve their freedom. They deserve a fair hearing. And they deserve to come home. We cannot let this go unanswered. If we don't stand up now, more and more police officers will suffer the same fate: thrown to the wolves by political activists in prosecutor's clothing. Josh and Brandon were railroaded, plain and simple. And now we need to right this wrong. Will you step up today and donate to support their appeals? Stand With Josh & Brandon Every dollar goes directly to their legal defense and appeals. Help bring them home. Your generous gift goes to defending these officers and restoring justice. No family should have to go through this. No officer should be imprisoned for doing his job. Let's send a message: we still back the badge—and we won't let politics destroy the lives of our heroes. For justice, Sheriff Joe Arpaio (ret.) National Center for Police Defense | Defenders for Justice

Written as plain as day on her website, my opponent is desperate and publicly calling for a flood of dark money to come in and discredit my record. But my agenda looks different. My record speaks for itself: I have consistently found ways to improve our region and our country. I’ve championed legislation that supports working families. name: Hakeem Jeffries
subject: Trump just called for my “impeachment”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
| I won’t mince words: Donald Trump just called for my “impeachment.”

What a complete joke. Here’s my response to the dumbest President ever to occupy the White House: Pipe down.

**I’m asking you to have my back right now and help me prove that I’m not going anywhere.**

[I’m calling for a historic surge of Democratic support — 5,000 donations in the next 24 hours — to make Trump’s worst nightmare a reality: A Democratic House that will stop him at every turn and END his reign of terror on the American people. Will you have my back and split $10 between me and the Voter Protection Project in this monumental moment to make Trump’s attack completely backfire and hand him the crushing blow he so desperately deserves?](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/109383?t=6&akid=24331%2E878179%2Eezjr6U)

Donald Trump is pathetic.

He can see the writing on the wall. Poll after poll shows Democrats in the lead for the House.

He’s terrified of what’s coming, so he’s attacking my credibility as the Democratic Leader.

He just can’t get me out of his head.

**Allow me to repeat myself for the President who is clearly suffering from Jeffries Derangement Syndrome:_Do not come for me unless I send for you._**

Let me make something very clear.

Once Democrats reclaim the Majority, we will be Trump’s worst nightmare.

His refusal to address the cost of living crisis? Handled.

His lawless Department of Homeland Security? Reined in.

His attacks on Social Security, SNAP and Medicare? Gone.

His reckless war of choice? Over.

[I know that if we band together, we will destroy Trump’s unhinged and corrupt trifecta. This is our moment to make our voices heard louder than ever before. I’m asking you to split $10 between me and the Voter Protection Project to send Trump and his Republicans an unmistakable message: Start packing. If we can reach 5,000 donations in the next 24 hours, Trump’s majority will be as good as over. name: MAGA MELTDOWN
subject: “This is NOT supposed to be a close race!”
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY RESIST RECLAIM REBUILD PAC (WWW
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-jamie [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-jamie]
Let me be honest with you about something.
I'm not supposed to be in this race.
The grandson of a sharecropper? Not supposed to be a viable Senate candidate in Louisiana.
A Black farmer from rural Tensas Parish? Not supposed to compete statewide in the Deep South.
Someone without billionaire backers or a Rolodex full of Washington insiders? Not supposed to have a shot at flipping a Senate seat.
But here we are.
And MAGA is absolutely shocked.
They thought Louisiana was locked up. They thought they could focus on defending seats in swing states while the South stayed safely red. They thought a race like ours — a real populist challenge to their grip on power — wasn't possible here.
They were wrong.
Look at what's happening: Democrats flipping Trump districts across the South. MAGA candidates tearing each other apart in primaries. Republican retirements at levels not seen since the Great Depression. Trump's approval cratering over his failed Iran war.
This is a once-in-a-lifetime opening. The kind of moment that doesn't come around often in Louisiana politics.
And I believe — deep in my bones — that this is the campaign to meet this moment.
Not because I'm the most polished politician. Not because I've been planning a Senate run my whole career. But because I understand what working Louisianans need because I've lived their struggles.
I know what it's like to watch costs rise while your income stays flat. I know what it's like to compete against corporations that rig the system against family operations. I know what it's like to worry about whether there will be anything left for the next generation.
That's the authentic economic populism that can win here. Not the fake MAGA version that delivers nothing but culture wars — the real thing that actually fights for working people.
And if we capitalize on this moment — if we show the political world that Louisiana is genuinely winnable — everything changes.
Not just for this race. For the entire map. For what's possible in the South.
But only if we have the resources to compete. And that counts on good folks like you stepping up.
Will you chip in $50, $25, or $10 to help us seize this once-in-a-lifetime opportunity? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-jamie]
CHIP IN TO FLIP THIS SENATE SEAT! >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-jamie]
We have the opportunity to pull off something historic in Louisiana that will do a whole lot of good for people here. I couldn’t be more grateful to have you fighting alongside me.
Jamie Davis
Democrat for U.S. Senate

subject: We Need Your Help! Let's Win This Thing Together!
body:
| | | | | [|
---](https://us.list-manage.com/PcU7H5rwQ8L?e=44433285d9&c2id=6eaf6a016b82ae70e238286ebc621ab8)
| | | | | | ,
---
| | We’re now 22 days until ballots roll out and **we need your help!** The only way we beat corporate power is with people power, so sign up for a volunteer event in the next two weeks!
---
| | 📞 We’re holding phonebanks to reach out and get the word about about Nate’s campaign. Join us **Tuesday, Wednesday, and/or Thursday between 5:30 PM and 7:30 PM** either virtually or in person in Sugar House!
---
| [Phonebank For Nate!](https://us.list-manage.com/GMcsjq2_3VW?e=44433285d9&c2id=6eaf6a016b82ae70e238286ebc621ab8)
---
| |
**🚪** The most important way you can get involved is through knocking doors, and it’s the most fun too! Join us for a weekday event at the same time and place as our phonebanks, on **Saturday, May 16th between 10:00 AM and 12:00 PM at Sunnyside Park,** or on **Saturday, May 23rd at Poplar Grove Park** at the same time.
---
| [Join Us At Sunnyside Park](https://us.list-manage.com/GNwoyhltiSj?e=44433285d9&c2id=6eaf6a016b82ae70e238286ebc621ab8)
---
| [Join Us At Poplar Grove](https://us.list-manage.com/KCYju27rQ_c?e=44433285d9&c2id=6eaf6a016b82ae70e238286ebc621ab8)
---
| |
Thank you so much to everyone who helped get us this far. This is the progressive campaign with the coalition to take on the establishment, powered by people, not corporate dollars. With your help, we can send a true progressive fighter to DC. If you have any questions, don't hesitate to reach out.
In Solidarity,Rowan Bracken (She/Her)Digital OrganizerNate Blouin For Utah

name: Judicial Watch Tipsheet
subject: Judicial Watch Victory: Arizona Appeals Court Rules State AG Kris Mayes’ Office Failed to Follow Law in Public Records Lawsuit
body:
Judicial Watch announced recently that the Arizona Court of Appeals overturned a lower court’s decision that allowed Arizona State Attorney General Kris Mayes to hide documents about its anti-Trump legal actions with a controversial outside left-wing group. The decision cited failures by the Mayes’ office to both properly search for and justify the secrecy of records concerning its coordination with States United Democracy Center.

clean_body: Judicial Watch announced recently that the Arizona Court of Appeals overturned a lower court’s decision that allowed Arizona State Attorney General Kris Mayes to hide documents about its anti-Trump legal actions with a controversial outside left-wing group. The decision cited failures by the Mayes’ office to both properly search for and justify the secrecy of records concerning its coordination with States United Democracy Center. | | | | | TOP STORIES | May 11, 2026 ---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | MUST READ MUST WATCH | | | | THE FITTON REPORT | | | | | | | | FOLLOW US | | | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|---|--- ---|---|---|---|---|---|---|--- | Judicial Watch, Inc. 425 3rd St SW Ste 800, Washington, DC 20024 | © 2026 | |

| | It would have sounded unthinkable a year ago, **but one of Democrats' best chances to gain a Senate seat in 2026 is Alaska.** Mary Peltola has run a rigorous, inspiring, and grassroots-driven campaign to lift up working people and take on a corrupt, unequal political system. **It's working.** A new poll has her leading by _six points_. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/131217978/620373848/2070068883?refcode=2026-05-11-ak&recurring=0&amount=4&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=eb5b31f4-a14d-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTQxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlYjViMzFmNC1hMTRkLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8wNDga9YQGVapO0lnv6t6jxXSNmWJVXn72RR4oyfRFQ=) But we can't look at a good poll and become complacent. In fact, this is a sign that we need to rally behind Mary **now** — because polls don't win elections. Plus, when Senate Republicans and their dark money backers see these polls, they realize that they'll need to invest in Alaska early. So Mary will soon face attacks as they spend big to try and stop her. To fight back, we need to help Mary finish out the month in as strong a position as possible. clean_body: That’s what a new poll suggests! | | | | It would have sounded unthinkable a year ago, but one of Democrats' best chances to gain a Senate seat in 2026 is Alaska. Mary Peltola has run a rigorous, inspiring, and grassroots-driven campaign to lift up working people and take on a corrupt, unequal political system. It's working. A new poll has her leading by six points. = But we can't look at a good poll and become complacent. In fact, this is a sign that we need to rally behind Mary now — because polls don't win elections. Plus, when Senate Republicans and their dark money backers see these polls, they realize that they'll need to invest in Alaska early. So Mary will soon face attacks as they spend big to try and stop her. To fight back, we need to help Mary finish out the month in as strong a position as possible. = Donate Now If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll split your contribution between Mary Peltola and Ron Wyden instantly: | = | = ---|--- = | = = Thank you, Ron | | Paid for and authorized by Wyden for Senate. | This email was sent by Wyden for Senate P.O. clean_body: | | | | | Friend, Did you see the recent Colorado Politics article? Here’s the headline: “Colorado congressional elections see over $9M in ad reserves from House Democrats’ super PAC” This means that our race has been singled out as one of the Democrats’TOP TARGETSanywhere in the country. National Democrats and outside special interests are preparing to pour more than $9 million to try and silence common-sense leadership fighting for Colorado. And I’ll be honest with you, Friend: We don’t have the money in our campaign account to match that kind of spending on our own. | | | | Right now, I am calling on all of my friends and supporters. The only way we win this fight is together— CO-03 should be decided by the people of Western Colorado—not by Washington Democrats and out-of-state donors trying to buy this seat. But whether we like it or not, the attacks are coming. Thank you,Jeff | | | | | ---|---|--- | | Paid for by Jeff Hurd For Congress Copyright © 2026 Jeff Hurd For Congress Our mailing address is: Jeff Hurd For Congress 2695 Patterson Rd, Suite 2-287 Grand Junction, CO 81506 If you would like to donate online, please visit our website . Be sure to follow the campaign on , , , and . You can also reach us at mailto:info@jeffhurdforcolorado.com.

| CalMatters just released new reporting confirming what we’ve been saying: _“The road to Congress runs through California.”_ They’re right. California will likely decide who controls the U.S. House in 2026 — and with _less than one month_ until the June 2 Primary Election, we need to ramp up organizing IMMEDIATELY. If we get outmaneuvered in key swing districts next month, Republicans could gain a massive advantage before voting in the General Election even begins. That’s why the California Democratic Party is urgently scaling up: ➡️ Grassroots organizing in battleground districts;

clean_body: California is a swing state (for the U.S. House) | = | CalMatters just released new reporting confirming what we’ve been saying: “The road to Congress runs through California.” They’re right. California will likely decide who controls the U.S. House in 2026 — and with less than one month until the June 2 Primary Election, we need to ramp up organizing IMMEDIATELY. Republicans and dark money groups are already flooding battleground districts with attacks because they know California could decide the House Majority. = = = Here’s the reality: California’s “top two” primary system means Democrats cannot afford to wait until November to organize. If we get outmaneuvered in key swing districts next month, Republicans could gain a massive advantage before voting in the General Election even begins. That’s why the California Democratic Party is urgently scaling up: ➡️ Grassroots organizing in battleground districts; ➡️ Spanish-language voter phonebanks; ➡️ Voter protection programs and poll monitoring ahead of the primary; ➡️ Canvassing and direct voter contact statewide. Even political reporters are acknowledging what we’ve known all along : California is ground zero for taking back the House. = = = = = = \- California Democrats | | = Our Democratic leaders are working hard to make California a better place to live. From fighting for good-paying jobs, combating climate change, to expanding healthcare access so all Californians can lead safe, healthy, prosperous lives — California’s Democratic leaders turn progressive ideas into meaningful action. name: Kshama for Congress
subject: Small businesses are being crushed by big corporations. Fight to elect Kshama Sawant!
body:
Come out on Thursday 5/14 to discuss, donate, and get involved!

| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/kshamasawant/socialists-for-kshama-13701692?e=bbdb764b3a)
---
| | | | [|
---](https://us.list-manage.com/kww9kpqpgKZ?e=bbdb764b3a&c2id=8b3f9b08d69d5a7a0bcc2cd67f268e15)
| | [**Small Businesses for Kshama Organizing Meeting**](https://us.list-manage.com/10laigqNKEI?e=bbdb764b3a&c2id=8b3f9b08d69d5a7a0bcc2cd67f268e15)
**Thursday, May 14th @ 6pm PT
**[**RSVP**](https://us.list-manage.com/1C8inugMn5L?e=bbdb764b3a&c2id=8b3f9b08d69d5a7a0bcc2cd67f268e15)**for the location**
---
| [RSVP NOW](https://us.list-manage.com/D2LWeyWL3E5?e=bbdb764b3a&c2id=8b3f9b08d69d5a7a0bcc2cd67f268e15)
---
| | Dear Peter,
My name is Shirley Henderson. I’m the co-owner of Squirrel Chops, a cafe and hair salon in Seattle’s Central District, and I’m proud to be fighting to elect independent socialist Kshama Sawant to Congress! The truth is that capitalism doesn't support small businesses. It's a system that favors the massive corporations, the ultra wealthy, the billionaires and centi-millionaires. Struggling small business owners have far more in common with the working people than they do with billionaires and big business.
Small businesses and working people alike are facing ever-increasing rents, major inflation in the cost of goods, and egregious healthcare and health insurance costs. So many small businesses that bring vibrancy and culture to neighborhood communities are being forced to close or are being displaced. This is especially true for women and minority-owned businesses.
**That is why a growing group of small business owners are fighting to elect socialist Kshama Sawant to the U.S. Congress.**
Kshama is the ONLY candidate this year who is fighting for small businesses like mine. Her campaign is calling for:
* Commercial and Residential Rent Control
* Free Healthcare for All - Tax the Rich
* Building Millions of Affordable Homes
* Ending all military aid to Israel

If you are a small business that is excited about these demands, join us at the Small Businesses for Kshama fundraiser on May 14th at 6pm to build the fight for commercial rent control and for Kshama’s historic campaign! RSVP [here](https://us.list-manage.com/mU2gwuwBV42?e=bbdb764b3a&c2id=8b3f9b08d69d5a7a0bcc2cd67f268e15) for more information including the location.
The Democratic and Republican parties say they support small businesses, but in reality, they carry water for Wall Street. In contrast, Kshama has a powerful track record of fighting for both working and poor people and for struggling small businesses. **And she knows how to win!** Kshama proved this while she was on the Seattle City Council for ten years from 2014 through 2023, where she won unprecedented victories like the highest minimum wage in the nation and the Amazon Tax to fund affordable housing.
**In 2016 Kshama also won an unheard-of $650,000 for small businesses, many of them owned by immigrants and people of color, as compensation for the losses they suffered due to construction disruption along the Central District's 23rd Avenue.** Kshama's office overcame the opposition of the City Council Democrats to win this.
Kshama organized a summit with small business owners throughout the city to develop a platform of progressive demands for small businesses. And she has been consistent in her fight for commercial rent control for small businesses, to stop the skyrocketing rents demanded by huge real estate corporations, which continuously pressures favorite local establishments to close.
Democrats on the other hand try to use small businesses as a cover to attack workers on behalf of big business. And this is not unique to Seattle. Across the country Democrats play this same destructive game. During the Great Recession it was the Democrats who bailed out the big banks and let working people and small businesses suffer the consequences of foreclosures and bankruptcies. When small businesses were suffering under the COVID lockdown, the Republicans and Democrats sent the vast majority of aid subsidies to big businesses. Neither Seattle nor Washington D.C. Democrats lifted a finger to stand up for small businesses.
Kshama’s opponent, Democratic Congressman Adam Smith, is no exception to the rule of the pro-corporate, war mongering Democratic Party. He is a 29-year incumbent bankrolled by big business, AIPAC, and the weapons industry. He voted for tens of billions of dollars of funding for the genocide in Gaza, and to block food aid to Gaza while millions of Palestinians starved. Smith voted for the Iraq War, to create ICE, and to bail out the Wall Street banks and big business in 2008.
Just as working people in our city, state and country need to organize to win social and economic justice, small businesses also have to come together, alongside workers, to fight back against a political establishment that serves the interests of big business. We need to stand with proven fighters like Kshama.
Join us at the Small Businesses for Kshama fundraiser on May 14th at 6pm!
Solidarity,Shirley HendersonCo-owner of Squirrel Chops
---
|
---

Help build our independent movement to shut down ICE and stop the deportations, for a $25/hour minimum wage, to end U.S. military aid to Israel, for free healthcare for all by taxing the rich, for national rent control, and for a new party for the working class!
[**Donate $25, $100, or $500 now!**](https://us.list-manage.com/Hx_8uWOjLkX?e=bbdb764b3a&c2id=8b3f9b08d69d5a7a0bcc2cd67f268e15)
---

---
| | | **Volunteer to build our fighting grassroots campaign!**
We have a real shot to win this campaign, but the Democratic Party will fight tooth and nail to keep Kshama out of Congress because they know she is a real fighter for working people. We need everyone to get involved to get the word out in order to defeat them!
[**Sign up to volunteer with our campaign today!**](https://us.list-manage.com/sfQkZnHUEWM?e=bbdb764b3a&c2id=8b3f9b08d69d5a7a0bcc2cd67f268e15)

name: Harry Dunn
subject: devastating defeat
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Harry Dunn for Maryland
body:
| _You're receiving these emails because you've shown past support for Harry Dunn and electing strong Democrats across the country. But if you'd like to unsubscribe, you can do so [here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/4-GOvCkgL02POgyixBF3xQ2?unsubscribedata=psREiuf9oHaRuMOZFeE1cM4lPPfW1t4fAEfKA4DSJq3FYuwh1PzjgoHffCfacwXzfDjVzA6KTnHzMN1SzIOr9IdAyufagxKhJI%2Fp0kR%2BTSaxHcnUHivwSZtvMQhKS%2FbsMfciKoaRWpiDrvAEN3IXRsVSGjGUhthEFdovOqofjMJ6PAMRv%2BvJ2US8TS%2BkR8N50Ga7JmKrtclavOlAq8sds1h96nw2zZku08nsjCq%2BgK2l5OHWbubxrVq4QzuAjZAlkQ197nitDOpnTT1renxUXt%2B6cHookt5qOXPWVipP4yvuYaaBGeC%2BWqZf%2BNhlYKHyO20O5eDZ6Vu05iT%2FEPQymHwQqkwHj%2Bngntf7iXASDpUFNBJQkKckPs6MN%2F4OZR9VreQooP7aBH%2FUJijXMJWDqWpmTTNi6xboiKjfGh8gs%2BImJmlQOQlxemUVIqcWkCWu)_ | [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/131218168/620376050/-1914144562?source=LOC_EM_FR_2026.05.11_I-Lost_B1_F1_S1_C1_IA_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTAzODYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOTBkMmI5NzktYWE0ZC1mMTExLThlZjItMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=LEHbsMEnym1fkv3uUgol8arWxetlkPF_mk3_m2a1XC4=)
---
I'm going to tell you something most candidates won't say out loud... **I lost.** [**I'll get into it more below, but I've learned not to wait on the ask — because the truth is, this campaign lives or dies on whether grassroots supporters like you are behind us. Dark money is moving fast. We have to move faster. So please, chip in $5 or $10 to help me win in 2026:**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/131218169/620376051/1131913799?amount=10&amounts=10,25,50,100,250&source=LOC_EM_FR_2026.05.11_I-Lost_B1_F1_S1_C1_IA_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTAzODYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOTBkMmI5NzktYWE0ZC1mMTExLThlZjItMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=LEHbsMEnym1fkv3uUgol8arWxetlkPF_mk3_m2a1XC4=) | **[JOIN THE FIGHT](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/131218170/620376052/1131913799?amount=10&amounts=10,25,50,100,250&source=LOC_EM_FR_2026.05.11_I-Lost_B1_F1_S1_C1_IA_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTAzODYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOTBkMmI5NzktYWE0ZC1mMTExLThlZjItMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=LEHbsMEnym1fkv3uUgol8arWxetlkPF_mk3_m2a1XC4=)**
---
In 2024, I ran for Congress in Maryland. I fought as hard as I knew how. I knocked on doors, made calls, and connected with voters.
The voters who heard my message? They were with me.
_The problem was getting heard at all._
Dark money donors spent MILLIONS of dollars to drown me out, and it worked. I watched it happen in real time — ads I couldn't match, mailers I couldn't answer, and a wall of money between me and the voters I was trying to reach.
When you have that kind of money, you don't have to win the argument. You just have to make sure the other side can't be heard.
I could've walked away. God knows it would've been easier.
But I filed to run again.
**I stood inside the United States Capitol on January 6th and watched Donald Trump's mob try to tear our democracy apart with their bare hands. I held the line that day. I testified. I fought back with everything I had.**
And I'll be damned if I let dark money and MAGA intimidation knock me down.
I'm running for this district, for this state, and for the idea that our democracy is worth defending even when it's hard. Especially when it's hard.
That means standing up to Donald Trump. It means standing up to the billionaires and the PACs and the anonymous money that's quietly buying our elections out from under us.
_It means being exactly the kind of elected official that corporate donors are spending all that money to keep out._
[**But look, if I’m going to compete with the billionaires and come out on top, I need grassroots Democrats like you on my team. clean_body: | You're receiving these emails because you've shown past support for Harry Dunn and electing strong Democrats across the country. But if you'd like to unsubscribe, you can do so | = I'm going to tell you something most candidates won't say out loud... I lost. = | = In 2024, I ran for Congress in Maryland. I fought as hard as I knew how. I knocked on doors, made calls, and connected with voters. The voters who heard my message? They were with me. The problem was getting heard at all. Dark money donors spent MILLIONS of dollars to drown me out, and it worked. I watched it happen in real time — ads I couldn't match, mailers I couldn't answer, and a wall of money between me and the voters I was trying to reach. When you have that kind of money, you don't have to win the argument. You just have to make sure the other side can't be heard. I could've walked away. God knows it would've been easier. But I filed to run again. I stood inside the United States Capitol on January 6th and watched Donald Trump's mob try to tear our democracy apart with their bare hands. I held the line that day. I testified. I fought back with everything I had. And I'll be damned if I let dark money and MAGA intimidation knock me down. I'm running for this district, for this state, and for the idea that our democracy is worth defending even when it's hard. Especially when it's hard. That means standing up to Donald Trump. It means standing up to the billionaires and the PACs and the anonymous money that's quietly buying our elections out from under us. It means being exactly the kind of elected official that corporate donors are spending all that money to keep out. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Let's do this. name: Daniel Cameron
subject: It's past midnight and I can't sleep.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cameron for Senate, Inc
body:
It's late and everybody on the campaign team is asleep. I probably should be too.

| | | | | |
---
| | [](https://eot.cameronforkentucky.com/f/a/gdHmboOlKjn3RNXzkCXtVA~~/AAAHURA~/wbLa6mjtQRl-c09LeXprMrRBVsxcuuRLdMyd-6UdEnpO9u8dmlI5DTTvrscN0rawd370TSxgBfm__wr3maJJOSqSxPARYJ_6Jm93q_M2WtNB54MHnHXVjMzMhsxrPnb-70qBNLRHAK6lYDYh2tXLC4_f3b4FI0Wle0OWUp5DgTk5a97a14R0kglr0_gLV4-a9HHHbYc18-m0R1r6dRwoqdJoKZI81UF5KGQ0oE5EOdpdjiZsOJWnbI_KGqRyTf8n)
---
Friend,
It's late and everybody on the campaign team is asleep.
I probably should be too. We've got an early start tomorrow, another full day of stops, more handshakes, pictures and conversations with people who are wondering if anyone in politics actually hears them anymore.
But I can't sleep. So I figured I'd write to you instead.
I've been sitting here thinking about my boys. Theodore, Bennett, Harrison. They're young enough that they really have no idea what this race is about. They won’t for many years.
I think about what I want them to inherit. Not money. Not a title. I want them to grow up in a country where a man can still stand on his convictions and run straight at a fight, even when the odds are stacked, even when the powerful line up against him - and still have a shot. I want them to know that America is still that place.
**That's why I'm running so hard, with a singular focus on May 19th.**
Not for a career or a political party or some big donors.
**For my boys. For my family. For yours. For the country we both know this still can be.**
Each day brings us closer to May 19th. I'm asking you to stay in this fight with me.
[_**Whatever you can give tonight - $10, $25, $100 - means more than you know.**_](https://eot.cameronforkentucky.com/f/a/gdHmboOlKjn3RNXzkCXtVA~~/AAAHURA~/wbLa6mjtQRl-c09LeXprMrRBVsxcuuRLdMyd-6UdEnpO9u8dmlI5DTTvrscN0rawd370TSxgBfm__wr3maJJOSqSxPARYJ_6Jm93q_M2WtNB54MHnHXVjMzMhsxrPnb-70qBNLRHAK6lYDYh2tXLC4_f3b4FI0Wle0OWUp5DgTk5a97a14R0kglr0_gLV4-a9HHHbYc18-m0R1r6dRwoqdJoKZI81UF5KGQ0oE5EOdpdjiZsOJWnbI_KGqRyTf8n)
I'm going to try to get some sleep. Big day tomorrow.
– Daniel
---

name: Senator Tom Willis
subject: West Virginia — Today, We Vote for Term Limits
body:
| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/b2166d5f26d7/west-virginia-today-we-vote-for-term-limits?e=a0285cc040)
---
|
---
| | | | | |

# West Virginia —
# Today, We Vote for Term Limits

---
|
---
|
---
| |
Dear Friend,

**Today is Election Day.**
This is it.
Everything comes down to whether you show up.
**Today, West Virginia votes for term limits.
Today, we retire the career politician.
Today, we elect Tom Willis.**
The political establishment thinks this race is over.
They think you’ll stay home.
They think nothing will ever change.
**Prove them wrong.**
For 26 years, I served this country as a Special Forces Green Beret **in the West Virginia National Guard**.
I built a business.
Practiced law.
Raised a family.
Lived in the real world — not inside the Washington political machine.
**I’m not a career politician.**
I’m one of you.
Meanwhile, Shelley Moore Capito has spent a quarter-century in Washington putting the establishment ahead of West Virginia families.
**West Virginians deserve a senator who fights for them — not the political class.**
West Virginians deserve leadership that fights for:
* Term limits
* Secure borders
* Fiscal responsibility
* The Second Amendment
* Religious liberty
* The right to life

**We are done with weak Republicans.** Done with insider politics.
Done being ignored.
**West Virginia doesn’t belong to the political class.**
It belongs to the people.
Polls will be opening soon.
**Go vote.****
**Take your family.
Take your friends.
Bring every conservative you know.
**If you show up, we win.**
**Vote Tom Willis TODAY.**
**IT’S TIME FOR A CHANGE, WEST VIRGINIA!**
Sincerely,
**Tom Willis**
---

subject: Trump to China, Biden Battle, and Memorial Day Travel

## Trump Heads to Beijing for Xi Summit
President Trump is set to arrive in Beijing on Wednesday for two days of talks with Chinese President Xi Jinping, the [first U.S. presidential visit to China](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGunTAMhp8GNqPEkBMYMnRhu107ohxiTlKFBAX3prx9Ba_QzbLlT__3__pavmyIIX1-2p0Mb3lJVM9IzFQWLn_2g_PqQ7Lv4Ci9LXMkmxYU-AKhQOLibIjXMrTObERIfUtGaj2qUahBtt7QJHs5otqmYZxWN7itJ-z1W7_0axqda4O5aUJJlEKMSndSK6m0U26w1k6TaAZBLnAu3e8cEnva4pW_qXRr3ttoPPNxNv2PBucG51prd1mf831tcL517nUu0TU428JhjdTg_MjBaa8TfDiBSziAMzyyUEOM8CZ4Jgc5EVR_gacTPjmkD6RcAWxyUL3l-4_-HrQySBSjQoVD53mPbTHuuFnhfOlmEJ_dhvjE5v_q-tvgvwAAAP__-EqUEw) since Trump's 2017 trip. Trump is bringing [16 U.S. executives](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2O4zAMBeDTyB0DmZFsuXCRxt1sO6XBWEysHf0YMhNvbr9IrjAtCTy8931_zV8UYsj3P5R4lFuZMx97ZBGus9RH2qQsa8h0DZ7zlUQiU55RYwfaQouzpxBfs2n8eGNGPjc8tn3vrNPWtM06dqQ74zvHV309D8N5cNo6Rmc63xlDugnjO03bFlutne1PbW9b23vrDRENg1ZGsw9S6ulvCVlWvsVXeXI9LSU1cVxFtl2dLwonhdNxHKe05_dP4cQZHrvC6T1K4VRk5fo-x5IhPfYfoOxBQoKllB-gVPIdFi478L-NF2EPUoCWpaSN8gs-IFAyfEhAatgUTlThckH8duiaOvrtCDGGveuV0fdEIX56yq9wnyP-DwAA___Qjo5u), including Elon Musk, Tim Cook, and Larry Fink, as he and Xi reportedly discuss a possible deal that would allow China to invest $1 trillion in U.S. factories. ## Biden Pushes to Block Release of DOJ Tapes
Former President Biden is moving to block the Justice Department from releasing [roughly 70 hours of partially redacted](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHSoyAURp9GOxwgIFJQbGOXbbd0rnCNJCgO3MTN2_-TvMJffzPfnHP-XacrxBT321_Y0NGSpx3PmpAIy0TluR2U_Rp3mGPAfQaihLBPksuecc2EnALE9J5UG9yCKPHSohPGDHrgWol2dQs3fd9bpby3A1gL1mhvUJjhYvUyqza6zxvXQgrOB206YbTQJuigAMBa3iiOIVIu3T3HnVZc0ju_sHQ-b21yK9FRm8ufRo6NHM_z7Jb8_2Px2Rs5HjlFir42cvxKsIr4qGxO2T9YyHdWMCFUZJILw-AZYmY-Pwux5VuGVXjXtrhwnDGlWHvTKH7bIKYvAP2q2svJnwAAAP___i-CHA) audio recordings of his 2017 conversations with his memoir ghostwriter. The recordings, gathered by Special Counsel Robert Hur during his investigation into Biden's handling of classified documents, are now being sought by the conservative Heritage Foundation through a Freedom of Information Act lawsuit. The audio includes Biden reading from notebooks later found to contain classified information, though Biden has maintained he never disclosed classified material to the ghostwriter. The DOJ set today as the deadline for Biden to take legal action, agreeing to delay any release until June 15 if he does. A Biden spokesperson said the tapes were provided to Hur on the condition that [they not be made public](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkMGOozAQRL_G3BzZDY7DwYeMVtyy0moPe0Qd3IAjYyO7Z9D8_Yr8wpxLVar3_j3GB4YY0vIbN3I85zHRUSMxUxm5fG4752kNCZ_BU3oicyRMIyi4SmWkhtFjiN9j13g3EwG1DTlt7c3clOl0szrdI6Fq9c0DGT9ZvE6zsd2k-iegV7oJ7lxTRoNW6mbsRVujjfXGd4jY90p0inzgXC6vHBKvNMfv_EXlMuWtiW5l3qto7wIGAcNxHJetpjMTMFCSn1XAcEIJGDKvVAQMbxjJWc5hWVn6_JJVFoqElWSe5bLmykcJTEUy7nR2scj7HeDP4-Ml2iFPwYv218dpjo76l8reFOf3I8QY6tWKTi0bhvj-yD8S--XgfwAAAP__cgKNkw), arguing that the push to release them is politically motivated rather than a genuine transparency effort.
## Eurovision Kicks Off amid Historic Boycott
The [70th Eurovision Song Contest](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD3O2zAQRE8jdTRIShTlgoUbdU6RJqWwJlfWBvwxyLUF3z5QrvBVUzzgYWb-3Nc7UKT8_AUJHW9lzXi0iMxYV67v9OLid8rwoID5AcwRIa9a6klII5ReA1D8rmMf3IaocejRKWtnM0szqn53g54GD6D8_Jgmaz346yjNpKb5KkHi1pM7bdIoraScjb0oa5SxwYQRAK5X2Y0SA3Gpl7-FMu-4xW_5YL34kvroduZX64Zbp5dOL8dxXFLLJ-v0glm8W6eXc9QJS43hzP0r8F3LhxqV3MQGMZY3i9MqqFXAKBJ8hd8hP1HwjsKX9EImppLFVip-sHZ6gSpuN61_p2Poqwuvg2KkNtlulM8EFP9X5B_9-nH6XwAAAP__UUCOeQ) kicks off today in Vienna, Austria, amid the biggest boycott in the music competition's history, with Israel's participation splitting European public broadcasters over the war in Gaza. Spain, Ireland, the Netherlands, Iceland, and Slovenia [withdrew from this year's contest](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD3u4yAQxU-DOyIYQ5wUFNvQ_bfd0sJmHM8KQwTjoNx-lVxhyyc9vY_fn5_5J1Ci_PgdDnS8lTljbwmZsc5cz-PJZd0ph4Ui5iUwJwx5BgVXqazUMMdA6T2bIboNEXAc0OlputmbskYPu9uW8WqsWdRo1qhs1EsYIy5RgVH3cbsO5D5pymrQSt3sdNGT1XaKNpoQwv2uhFEYiUu9_C2UecctvcsL62Utx5DczvxsYvwlwAvwvfcLVWo704HtYxHg1zPxWVGAP85GqwD_qRTglRXgNQjweNbyokYly--5vmOW1CSxLFn2vXzEUt5rYab8kCFHuZYzRUmtBkyyUxbgh-ris1NK1K6TMOpxBErfofxfdF8O_gUAAP__OmCOGw), while more than 1,000 musicians, including Brian Eno, Peter Gabriel, and Macklemore, signed an open letter calling for a boycott, and the 2024 Swiss winner, Nemo, returned the Eurovision trophy in protest. Israel's culture minister called the boycott "shameful and hypocritical," while Spanish broadcaster RTVE framed its withdrawal as a matter of "collective responsibility." Israel won the public vote in 2025 after accounts linked to the Israeli government urged supporters to vote the maximum allowed number of times. In response, organizers cut the [per-fan vote limit](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDFywyAURE-DOjyAQEgFRRp1TptS8yW-rJ8g0AC249tn5Cuk2WZn3-zu13W6AgWKt0_Y0dU1TRGfJWCtmKea7_tR07JRhJk8xhlqDQhxUkJ1XBgu1eSBwmvSjXcrosK2QSet7U0vjJbN5mw_exxEB3L1stVWzC0ssCy91q2aO2jInTRhpJJC9MZepDXSWG-8BoBhEEwL9FRTvnwninXDNbzSA_NlSXsT3FbrUVj7wdTI1Ij3nB6Jfk-TqfFEMzUKc8rA1PgDkVMpd_R8TXmHwJ-QI8UbP5H8HadCKfL3xiOnPVVKkamxyc4fTwqBSmeZFrcdKLxL1H8993DqLwAA__9XdIHa) in half and brought back professional juries for the semifinals.

### The making and unmaking of one gerrymandered district, and why most are still standing
| | [Democracy Watch](https://substack.com/@democracywatch1) and [The Angry Democrat](https://substack.com/@theangrydem)
---
| May 12
---
| [](https://substack.com/@democracywatch1)| | [](https://substack.com/@theangrydem)
---|---|---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3159258&post_id=197262733&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTk3MjYyNzMzLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc3ODU4MTIwOCwiZXhwIjoxNzgxMTczMjA4LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzE1OTI1OCIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.jhhnhRS1H6dpVJu84zB54M3b0qrPbkOI6NKyDjBtYQc&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3159258&post_id=197262733&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTk3MjYyNzMzLCJpYXQiOjE3Nzg1ODEyMDgsImV4cCI6MTc4MTE3MzIwOCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMxNTkyNTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.jkfBhab8dM1WS5qWiuGidPVh9858ldali6NchMRL9hk&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3159258&post_id=197262733&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTk3MjYyNzMzLCJpYXQiOjE3Nzg1ODEyMDgsImV4cCI6MTc4MTE3MzIwOCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMxNTkyNTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.jkfBhab8dM1WS5qWiuGidPVh9858ldali6NchMRL9hk)

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/mattdiemer/p/the-rigged-map-part-1-how-they-drew?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Before 2011, Pennsylvania's 7th congressional district was a place where elections actually mattered. The district covered most of Delaware County, the Philadelphia suburbs west and south of the city. From the mid-1970s through the late 1980s it elected a Democrat; from then through the mid-2000s, a Republican, Curt Weldon. In 2006, Navy vice admiral Joe Sestak defeated Weldon in a contested race. When Pat Meehan won the seat back in 2010, he did so by a competitive margin against a state representative named Bryan Lentz. That was a real district. Voters in it could plausibly affect its outcome. Both parties had to compete for it.
[Subscribe to The Angry Democrat](https://substack.com/redirect/431da652-386b-4e42-881f-e7e080a02512?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[Subscribe to The Angry Democrat](https://substack.com/redirect/431da652-386b-4e42-881f-e7e080a02512?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6f4e1d98-0d2d-4af3-a54f-a6627e1d9094?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**Congressional District 7 2000-2010**
A year later, the district ceased to exist.
What replaced it became, almost immediately, a national joke. The new 7th sprawled across five counties -- Delaware, Chester, Montgomery, Berks, and Lancaster -- connected by passages so narrow that at one point, in King of Prussia, the entire district squeezed through a strip of land barely wider than the parking lot of a steakhouse called Creed's Seafood and Steaks. _The Washington Post_ named it one of the ten most gerrymandered districts in America. The shape was widely compared to a Disney cartoon: Goofy kicking Donald Duck.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d6f2728b-49be-4c19-94d1-15751c91bc48?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Pennsylvania _7th Congressional District 2012_
The lines were not random. They **[cracked](https://substack.com/redirect/045ab6f0-4485-45c2-b2d8-b11a49c1cf7a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** Pennsylvania's fifth-largest city, Reading -- one of the poorest urban communities in the country -- across multiple districts, pairing its voters with Lancaster County farmers and rural Chester County landowners. The purpose was to dilute the Democratic-leaning voters of Delaware County by pairing them with enough rural Republican territory in four other counties that the aggregate vote in the new 7th would come out Republican by a comfortable margin -- regardless of how strongly the people in Delaware County voted. Their votes still counted. The map ensured they would be outnumbered.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a8dbb62e-4e66-4411-9b58-ab07c75e21e1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
It worked. Meehan won the new 7th with 60 percent of the vote in 2012, 62 percent in 2014, and 60 percent again in 2016. He never faced a competitive primary. He never faced a competitive general election. From the moment the new lines were enacted until he resigned in 2018 amid a sexual harassment settlement scandal, the seat was effectively uncontested -- not because Delaware County had stopped being purple, but because the map guaranteed that whoever the Republican primary chose would win the general.
This is how a district disappears. The people who lost were not the Democratic Party -- which survived 2012, 2014, and 2016 without difficulty. The people who lost were the residents of PA-7 themselves, Republican and Democrat and unaffiliated, whose representative no longer needed to listen to them because the map had decided he didn't have to. The same arrangement, drawn by Democrats in Illinois, produces the same result for voters in the packed Republican districts there. The party machinery on either side survives. The voters do not.
The story of how this happened begins not in Pennsylvania but in a Republican strategist's reading of the _New York Times_.
* * *
## The Plan
[Subscribe to Democracy Watch](https://substack.com/redirect/421b5c63-957e-4adc-b0db-6766e451dcad?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[Subscribe to Democracy Watch](https://substack.com/redirect/421b5c63-957e-4adc-b0db-6766e451dcad?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
In early 2009, Chris Jankowski, executive director of the Republican State Leadership Committee, came across an article noting that 2010 would be a census year. The next round of state legislative elections would determine who controlled congressional redistricting for the entire decade. Jankowski did the arithmetic. Eighteen state legislative chambers, he later said, had partisan margins of four seats or fewer. Those chambers, in those states, would decide American politics until at least 2022.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/df76faa1-7013-46ee-b7a9-526dbc195c5d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Chris Jankowski
The plan he developed was called REDMAP. It identified 107 state legislative races in 16 states -- Pennsylvania, Ohio, Michigan, Wisconsin, North Carolina, and Florida as priority targets -- with a fundraising goal of roughly $30 million. Trivial in federal politics; enormous in down-ballot races where a few hundred thousand dollars could swing an outcome. In March 2010, Karl Rove published an op-ed in the _Wall Street Journal_ endorsing the strategy. Its subhead read: _He who controls redistricting can control Congress_.
In Pennsylvania, REDMAP spent close to a million dollars on three state House races and won all three. Combined with the 2010 Republican wave, Republicans flipped the Pennsylvania House and the governorship while holding the Senate -- unified control of all three for the second consecutive redistricting cycle, this time with the strategic infrastructure to lock the new maps in place. The wave alone might have produced the same legislative majorities. What REDMAP added was the second prong: lawyers, mapping software, and operatives ready to draw districts that would survive future waves. Waves recede. Maps do not.
Drawing Pennsylvania's new congressional map happened largely behind closed doors. The legislature's Republican leadership released a redistricting bill in September 2011 that contained no map at all, only placeholder language. The actual map appeared in mid-December and was approved by the state Senate on a 26-24 party-line vote. Democratic state Senator Vincent Hughes pointed out the design. Of Pennsylvania's eighteen U.S. House districts, both plans would give Democrats roughly four safe seats. But the Republican plan would produce twelve safe Republican seats and just two genuinely competitive districts. The Democratic alternative would have produced eight safe Republican seats and six competitive ones. Even the Democratic counter-plan favored Republicans. The Republican plan was different in kind -- not designed to give Republicans a majority, but to eliminate competition.
The pattern that followed vindicated the design. Through this period, Pennsylvania had roughly a 10-point Democratic registration advantage. Across 2012, 2014, and 2016, Republicans held 13 of Pennsylvania's 18 House seats -- a 72 percent share, year after year, in a state Hillary Clinton lost by less than one percentage point and Obama carried twice. The single most revealing year was 2012. Pennsylvania that November reelected Obama by more than five points, reelected Democratic Senator Bob Casey Jr. by nearly nine, and gave Democratic congressional candidates a majority of votes cast statewide. They received five of eighteen House seats in return.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/13b9369d-d633-4f52-a408-6b224f235370?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Pennsylvania Congressional Maps pre-2018 PA Supreme Court Ruling
* * *
## The Cost
[Share The Angry Democrat](https://substack..-ClVxtgDv-2Qjvi66D30CNJPsWNd4MXF51VV5VMt76I?)
[Share The Angry Democrat](https://substack..-ClVxtgDv-2Qjvi66D30CNJPsWNd4MXF51VV5VMt76I?)
What this meant for voters inside districts like the 7th was a slow disappearance of representation. Retirees at Kendal-Crosslands, carved out of the 7th into a district whose representative lived an hour away in Lancaster County, told reporters their letters came back as canned form responses on issues they hadn't raised.
One wrote about health care. The reply thanked them for their interest in deer hunting.
By 2017, with constituent pressure cresting on the Trump administration and the ACA, Meehan declined to hold open in-person town halls, defending tele-town halls as "a very effective way to stay in touch." Aides screened the questions. There were no follow-ups. A representative whose seat is gerrymandered to win by twenty-plus points has no electoral incentive to face an angry crowd. The crowd cannot remove him. The map has already settled that.
Something else fills the accountability void. In a gerrymandered safe seat, only a primary can remove an incumbent -- and corporate money has colonized primaries too, funding incumbents and any challengers who might emerge against them. There is no longer a moment in the electoral cycle where an ordinary voter's preference reliably determines who represents PA-7. Accountability flows toward the funders. That is not a quid pro quo. It is gravity. Meehan's largest industry source of contributions over his career was Lawyers and Law Firms. The largest employment sectors for actual PA-7 residents, per the 2012 Census, were Educational Services and Health Care and Social Assistance: teachers, nurses, hospital staff, social workers. The people writing checks to keep him in Washington and the people whose work filled his district were not the same population.
* * *
## The Court
[Subscribe to Democracy Watch](https://substack.com/redirect/421b5c63-957e-4adc-b0db-6766e451dcad?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The 7th was not undone by an election. It was undone by a state Supreme Court. Gerrymanders are not permanent. They can be reversed.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e4fdb9df-a45e-4e60-92c7-b4ee9c27d4aa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
In June 2017, the League of Women Voters of Pennsylvania filed a state-court lawsuit alleging the 2011 map violated the Pennsylvania Constitution's Free and Equal Elections Clause. On January 22, 2018, the Pennsylvania Supreme Court agreed 5-2, ruling the map a "severe and durable" partisan gerrymander. When the legislature could not produce a remedial plan, the court drew one itself.
The new map was fair. Most of the old 7th became part of a new 5th covering all of Delaware County, and in November 2018, attorney Mary Gay Scanlon won it decisively against Republican Pearl Kim. Across the state, Pennsylvania's House delegation moved from 13-5 Republican to 9-9. Two cycles later, under another court-drawn map after the 2020 census, the same state elected 10 Republicans and 7 Democrats in 2024. A fair map moves with the voters. A gerrymandered map does not. That is what makes one rigged and the other not.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a09b6529-4930-46be-9c8f-70077a0f1ea8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**[Pennsylvania Supreme Court issues new congressional map for 2018 elections](https://substack.com/redirect/6513e0e4-d397-4f15-80f4-c68d02bad38f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
The catch is that this remedy was specific to Pennsylvania. The federal courts that voters in other states might have used are no longer available -- two recent Supreme Court rulings, _Rucho v. Common Cause_ in 2019 and _Louisiana v. Callais_ last month, closed the federal pathways for partisan-gerrymandering claims and for most challenges under the Voting Rights Act. That leaves only state court, under a state constitution, in the small number of states with strong enough language and a willing court.
Roughly a dozen states qualify; most states with the worst gerrymanders do not. North Carolina tried this same path in 2022 and won; then Republicans took the state supreme court that fall and reversed the ruling. Pennsylvania got lucky. Most states have not. And the path is itself under attack -- the same Republican organization that funded REDMAP in 2010 has spent more than $29 million since 2014 to elect conservative state supreme court justices in the states where the federal door is now closed. We return to that fight in Part 9.
The map did not happen by accident. It was conceived as a strategy, funded as an investment, executed as a campaign, and sustained as a structural advantage. It produced a representative who stopped needing the people he represented because the people who could remove him were elsewhere. Multiply that across roughly 340 congressional districts and hold it for a decade, and you have explained a substantial portion of why the United States Congress no longer responds to majorities of its own voters.
**In Part 2** , we follow the legal architecture as it was dismantled -- from _Buckley v. Valeo_ in 1976, through _Citizens United_ in 2010, through _Rucho_ in 2019, through the _Callais_ decision last month. The law is the second half of the machine.
## Stay Angry
